title: C++ Exception Handling
content:
### Exception Handling

Exceptions provide a way to transfer control from one part of a program to another when an error occurs. They separate error-handling code from normal code.

---

### Basic Concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `throw` | Signals an exception |
| `try` | Block that may throw |
| `catch` | Handles the exception |
| Stack unwinding | Destructors called as scope exits |

---

### Throwing Exceptions

```cpp
int divide(int a, int b) {
  if (b == 0) {
    throw "Division by zero";
  }
  return a / b;
}
```

---

### Standard Exception Classes

```cpp
#include <stdexcept>

throw std::runtime_error("Something went wrong");
throw std::logic_error("Invalid logic");
throw std::out_of_range("Index out of bounds");
```

---

### noexcept Specifier

```cpp
void safeFunc() noexcept {  // Guarantees no exception
  // Code that should never throw
}

void maybeThrows() noexcept(false);  // May throw (default)
```

---

### Exception Safety Levels

| Level | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| No-throw | Never throws |
| Strong | Operation succeeds or rolls back |
| Basic | No resource leaks, invariant preserved |
| Minimal | May have resource leaks |

---

> **Key Point:** Catch exceptions by const reference to avoid slicing. Never throw exceptions from destructors. Use RAII to ensure resources are properly cleaned up during stack unwinding.

quiz.question: What keyword is used to signal an exception?
